label: Baseline
title: Zero-radius MCA
summary: At zero distance the bad-line event is the two endpoint challenges where the affine line equals one of its defining source words.
relevance: Useful only as a denominator sanity check.

label: Safe
title: Below unique decoding
summary: CA and MCA coincide in the unique-decoding regime, and Reed-Solomon codes have small MCA error up to this boundary.
relevance: Known safe region, far below the current frontier radius.

label: Safe
title: Approaching Johnson
summary: The strongest general Reed-Solomon MCA upper bounds reach the Johnson radius with polynomial dependence on the reserve.
relevance: This is the positive benchmark we are already far beyond.

label: Obstruction
title: Approaching capacity
summary: Near capacity, known lower bounds show MCA/CA error must grow polynomially in n over the field-size denominator.
relevance: The current RS-MCA frontier lives in this capacity-edge lower-bound regime.
